description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who has just finished three model-training sessions.  
All experiment folders are already present under:

/home/user/experiments/

and each run is stored in its own sub-directory (e.g. run_2023-08-01, run_2023-08-02 …).  
Every run contains a mixture of:

• *.ckpt  – model checkpoints  
• *.log   – training / validation logs (plain text)  
• *.json  – metrics summaries (plain text)  

Goal
1. Collect every “*.ckpt” file that exists anywhere inside /home/user/experiments/ and move it into a new directory named /home/user/experiments/ckpt_archive/ (create it if it does not yet exist).  
   • Only one level of archive is required; do NOT recreate the original directory structure inside ckpt_archive.  
   • After the move, there must be **no** “*.ckpt” files left in any of the original run_* folders.  
2. Produce a comma-separated inventory that lists the absolute path and exact byte-size of **every regular file** now residing under /home/user/experiments/ (including the files you just moved).  
   • Name the file /home/user/experiments/artifact_inventory.csv  
   • CSV format – no spaces:  
     path,bytes  
   • Include a single header row exactly spelled path,bytes  
   • One subsequent line per file, sorted in pure lexicographical order by the absolute path.  
   • Example of row formatting (do NOT hard-code these values):  
     /home/user/experiments/some_folder/file.ext,123  
3. Write a two-line summary log called /home/user/experiments/cleanup.log with the following exact layout (each line terminated by “\n”):  
   CKPT_MOVED: N  
   INVENTORY_LINES: M  
   where  
   • N = total number of checkpoint files successfully moved to ckpt_archive  
   • M = total number of **data lines** (i.e. excluding the header) present in artifact_inventory.csv after you generate it.  

Constraints
• Accomplish all three goals using a combination of find and xargs (other coreutils such as mv, wc, sort, stat etc. are allowed).  
• Do not delete or rename any non-checkpoint files.  
• Do not manually type file names; rely on find/xargs so the procedure works even if more runs are added later.  
• Both artifact_inventory.csv and cleanup.log must reside directly inside /home/user/experiments/ and be world-readable (0644 is fine).  

The automated checker will validate:

1. That /home/user/experiments/ckpt_archive/ exists and contains **only** “*.ckpt” files.  
2. That every original run_* directory now contains zero “*.ckpt” files.  
3. The exact contents of /home/user/experiments/artifact_inventory.csv (header + lexicographically-sorted data rows with correct byte counts).  
4. The exact contents of /home/user/experiments/cleanup.log (correct numbers, correct spelling, correct line order).  

Use as many interactive shell commands as you need, but be sure that by the end of your session the file system matches the specification above.
truth:
